_comment: HTTP response envelopes from POST /api/ai/regenerate for each scenario.
UpdatedContent is a raw string — RegenerationService patches it into content[sectionIdentifier]
So for simple string sections (summary) it replaces the string value directly.
For complex sections (experience, skills) it replaces the whole node with a string.

SCENARIO_1_summary_response.updatedContent: AI-Polished [summary]: Cloud-focused Senior Software Engineer with 5+ years architecting high-availability .NET microservices on Azure. Delivered systems at 99.9% uptime, cut latency by 40%, and led teams of up to 6 engineers — results over résumé fluff.

SCENARIO_2_experience_response.updatedContent: AI-Polished [experience]: Rewrite all experience descriptions with stronger action verbs and quantified outcomes.
SCENARIO_2_experience_response._note: In the current mock implementation UpdatedContent is a plain string. RegenerationService stores it verbatim into content['experience'], replacing the array with a string. A real AI would return a JSON array string here.

SCENARIO_3_skills_response.updatedContent: AI-Polished [skills]: Reorder by relevance for a backend .NET role. Group related technologies.
SCENARIO_3_skills_response._note: After patching, content['skills'] becomes a string instead of an array. Frontend must handle both shapes, or the regenerate endpoint must be extended to handle structured sections.
